findings: stampede.sh has no watchdog or restart loop; it launches panes, captures PIDs once after a fixed 5s sleep, and exits launcher control.
Teardown kills tracked PIDs and the tmux session but does not requeue claimed tasks, so in-flight work can be orphaned.
Commander launch failures are recorded into swarm-state.json and child-agents.jsonl, but there is no retry or substitution path.
Each pane sleeps for 86400 after agent exit, so pane liveness can outlast worker liveness and confuse recovery diagnostics.
PID capture depends on a fixed 5s delay and may record the wrong process if the actual worker spawns late.
